LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF JANET GONG'S MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CSM-11-838382 WITH CASE CGC-11-515186. THE MOTION IS DENIED. TRIAL DATE HAS ALREADY BEEN SET AND CONTINUED TWICE IN THE SMALL CLAIMS CASE. THE COURT FINDS THAT CONSOLIDATION IS PREJUDICIAL TO DEFENDANTS AND WILL NOT AVOID UNNECESSARY COSTS OR DELAY. SEE CCP 1048(A). ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; REPORTER: LAVENA WARD, CSR #7077

MASTER CALENDAR MOTION ON JAN-04-2012 IN DEPT. 206, HEARING ON APPLICANT MIRIAM NALLEY'S WAIVER OF COURT FEES AND COSTS IS CONTINUED FROM JAN-04-2012 TO JAN-18-2012, 9:00 AM IN DEPT. 206, ON THE COURT'S OWN MOTION. (206)

REMOVED FROM DENIED FEE WAIVER PAID CALENDAR: NO FEES OWED BY DEFENDANT MIRIAM PAVLOVA NALLEY. (206)

Amended Notice Of Special Motion To Strike (Ccp 425.16); Memo P/A
Off Calendar Jan-03-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS BRIAN CABRALES AND MIRIAM NALLEY'S SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE (CCP 425.16).
Appearances: Teela Crosthwaite of Dimitriou & Associates PC (434.1144) for defendants and moving parties; no other appearances.
The court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: granted. No opposition filed. Attorney's fees are awarded in the amount of $4000. Order signed. (302/MJM) Judge: Marla Miller, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR #7077

Notice Of Motion And Special Motion To Strike
Off Calendar Dec-29-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling -
DEFENDANTS MIRIAM NALLEY AND BRIAN CABRALES' Special Motion To Strike is continued to January 3, 2012 per the amended notice of motion. Judge: Marla Miller, Reporter: La Vena Ward, CSR # 7077 =(302/MJM)

MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CSM-11-838382 WITH CASE CGC-11-515186
Off Calendar Nov-10-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Janet Gong, 415-254-1096, Plaintiff and moving party in Pro Per

No other Appearance. Discussed and Tentative Ruling is adopted. PLAINTIFF JANET GONG'S MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CSM-11-838382 WITH CASE CGC-11-515186.
The motion is Denied. Trial date has already been set and continued twice in the small claims case. The Court finds that consolidation is prejudicial to Defendants and will not avoid unnecessary costs or delay. See CCP 1048(a). Order signed in open court. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR #7077
